rulings.Case Title: ERIC W. LIEN VS. LUCKY UNITED PROPERTIES INVESTMENTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Supplemental Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 1 , 2011, Line 1 CROSS DEFENDANT ALBERT LEE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Supplemental Attorneys' Fees And Costs IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. LEE IS AWARDED $3,600 FOR OPPOSING LUCKY'S EX PARTE APPLICATION, $300 FOR WORK RELATED TO LUCKY'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE, $1,350 FOR BRINGING THIS MOTION, AND COSTS OF $105 WHICH INCLUDES A $40 FILING FESS FOR THIS MOTION AND A $65 COURT CALL FEE FOR THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION FOR A TOTAL AWARD OF $5355. LEE IS AWARDED NO FEES AND COSTS FOR WORK RELATED TO THE MOTION HEARD ON 12/8/2010 AS THIS COURT'S 1/31/11 ORDER ALREADY AWARDED LEE HIS FEES AND COSTS IN BRINGING THAT MOTION. THIS MOTION WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE ALVARADO AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE REMAINDER OF THE MARCH 1, 2011 302 CALENDAR. =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CONDENSA VS. MBV LAW, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For A Protective Order; Notice; Req For Judicial Notice; Notice Of Lodgement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 1, 2011. Line2. DEFENDANTS MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER AND PAUL NEIBERGS' Motion For A Protective Order. Motion for protective order is Granted. Defendants have met their preliminaty showing that the privilege exists and was held by Helio Alone. This privilege thasfered from Incentiva to Laurus per the Asset Purchase Agreement Section 2.1(m) to (n). The Attorney-client privilege was not waived when the documents were provided to former Directors of Helio because they failed to inform defendants that they no longer were Directors of Incentiva and without the consent of the current holder of the priviledge, Laurel. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: BUNZL DISTRIBUTION USA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 1, 2011, line 9, DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s demurrer is sustained in part, and overruled in part. As to the 3rd cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The cause of action is sufficiently plead and the confidentiality provisions of Rev. and Tax Code §19442 (g)(3) does not apply to the circumstances in this case. As to the 4th cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is barred under Govt. Code §860.2. As to the 5th cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff¿s allegation that there is a mandatory duty owed by the Plaintiff is sufficient to survive at the pleading stage. As to the 6th cause of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action is barred under Rev. and Tax Code §19442(i). =(302/LMG)
